threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' like a true local. And that hesitation when ordering a coffee at the market? Tells everyone you're fresh off the boat.
Last night on the 36, a guy got on at Peckham Rye, dressed like he just stepped out of a 90s rave. Gave an impromptu DJ set the whole ride to Elephant.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting in my kitchen, I heard a group of kids outside debating whether the BVG strike was worth skipping school. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a rooftop pool party happening right next to a graffiti-covered former factory. The vibe? Unmatched and totally Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their eyes rolling at BART delays while sipping a coffee from La Mediterranee. Tourists just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo juggling on the 49 while someone next to him argued about the price of avocado toast. Classic San Francisco vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think we all love the music festivals in Plaça del Nord, but ask any local about the noise at 3 AM on a Sunday.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll spot them struggling with the 'café con leche' order, still pronouncing it like 'ka-fay' instead of the local 'ka-feh'. It's the little slips that give them away.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused at bus stops, waiting for the bus to actually show up. Locals know it's just a suggestion, mate.
Caught a whiff of jerk chicken on the 333 last night; turns out the driver stopped mid-route for a takeaway. Classic Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using the tram tracks as a makeshift skateboard park, while the BVG was on strike, I knew this was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a self-service beer tap at a Späti. Grab a cup, pour your own, and sip while chatting with locals. No fuss!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they call it 'the 24' instead of the ‘24 bus’ and can list all the pizza spots without googling.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy reading Shakespeare on the 49 this morning. Perfectly calm while the rest of us stewed in traffic. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in d'Aritja Park are for show, but locals know they’re the best spot for chatting on a hot summer night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confounded look when faced with Gràcia's winding streets—like they've just exited a labyrinth.
